segments.text: Silver just moved 7% in a single session. Gold moved $50 overnight. And if you're like most traders on Twitter right now, you're getting crushed.
You're in a drawdown, a painful account threatening drawdown. You're losing money, you're losing your confidence, and you're starting to wonder if even belong in this market.
Stop. We have some good news. At SMB, we learn from these painful periods. We use them as a catalyst for growth.
Today, we're taking you inside our desk to show you the eight specific lessons our traders learned to survive this volatility.

And most importantly, we're giving you the cure to get back to green. I might be like, if you're a co-founder of SMB Capital, we've built seven and eight figure pre-ear traders by teaching them exactly what works when the market turns hard.
And if you're serious about protecting your capital and growing your account, you're in the right place. Let's get started.
Here are the eight lessons our traders have learned during a painful drawdown. Please stay tuned until the end for the cure.

Number one, don't miss what works best for you. Now, on our desk, I'll tell you an anecdote about our best team from 2025. This is our best team hands down based on risk adjuster returns based on overall PML based on work ethic, outperformance from other teams.
Number one team at SMB in 2025. And some of the traders on this team are in a painful drawdown mostly from recent events.
But Spencer, Steve Spencer, my partner has been walking around the trading floor bragging about this amazing seven figure breakout trade that they just made recently.
And how this was a great example of what they do best. But this team, these group of guys, they were pussing around in a new type of product.
They were trying out how to make money in a set of the technically looked really good for them in commodities relative to stocks, but they were trying something new.
And they were getting away from what they do best. And so oftentimes, and I'm giving you an example of some hiccups that these guys have had in commodities.
But we can we can replace commodities with something else. We can replace 2026 with 2027. We can replace 2026 Q1 with Q3.
These drawdowns, these periods of underperformance, these disappointments are going to happen to elite traders multiple times of the year.
They're going to happen to developing traders, newer traders. It happens to all of us. And the key during these drawdowns is to understand and learn.
And so number one, you're going to find whenever you're in a drawdown is you got to you got to double down on what you do best.
And you just can't miss what you do best. These guys break out trades. They can't miss breakout trades when they're hawking a new product.
You can't miss what you do best while you're trying to learn something new. Okay. Let's move on to number two.
Commodities don't trade like equities. Crypto doesn't trade like equities. Silver, gold doesn't trade like NVIDIA.
You need a separate playbook for each. Helic historically, and I'll use an example in silver.
We've had, we've had this extreme volatility in silver defined by moves of five percent or more.
And we've had a cluster of them recently. Silver historically maybe moves on average five to ten days per year on average five percent or more.
And the last 24 months, we've had these clusters of days. And so people have been drawn to this particular opportunity of late and twenty six silvers ATR.
It's average who range is tripled. And so, you know, while I'm using an example of silver, again, we can replace it with with something else.
The idea is when you're trading something very new, perhaps you're good in technology stocks and you're moving to a different sector in the marketplace.
They don't different sectors trade differently. Certainly commodities trade differently than equities.
Certainly growth stocks trade differently than legacy stocks.
You need different playbooks for the instrument for the stock for the product that you're trading.
And that in that in of itself can cause the underperformance number three when learning a new product or a new stock.
Earn your risk. Don't trade it with the same risk as your equities playbook.
You have a history of success. If you're now trading commodities for the first time prove you can handle the new product consistently add risk from success.
We had a team recently who had a drawdown in crypto and Steve Spencer, my partner made a really good point to the team that crypto doesn't trade like equities and you can't trade them like equities.
This really bright sharp smart team was learning this and didn't really understand this until talking to Spencer and learning about this through some pain.

But this is an example of why even if you're an elite trader good at something else, you got to start small in this new product or new stock and then bill from success to make sure you don't lose too much on a hard lesson.
All right, number four, this is not the market to be playing for the grants lab.
And I will share that recently we have made two separate mid-set seven figure trades in commodities in the past five trading sessions.
And we made another seven figure trade in commodities in the last five trading sessions and we didn't lock in profits.
We let them ride and we gave almost all of it back.
I'm not suggesting that we should have locked in all the profit, but we should have locked in at least half and we'll review these trades with our traders they agreed with that.
Lock in half wafer continued confirmation and then and then reenter there was a consensus that was the right thing to do.
And again, I'm talking about trading in commodities, but there's going to be times when you're playing for grants lambs in what seems to be an A plus opportunity.
And the reason for your underperformance is you're playing for a grants lab as opposed to taking some really solid.

Lessor gains and you know our guys in our desk were playing for a 10 million dollar trade and they if you if you look back on it, they could have made that they could have made that in three trades if they took their profits the way that they they do and a lot of other things, but they really went for it.
And you got to understand there's certain markets where that's just that's that's not really a good thing to do. There are markets for that, but this isn't one of them.
Number five, don't seed your structural edge in the market as active traders.
We can enter and exit much more easily than bigger players book your profits.
You can always reenter number six past success doesn't have to continue adjustments must be made in different market regimes.
And new products look you've got to as an elite trader when you become an elite trader.
When you become a consistently profitable traders this there's this idea that like now you can do what you want.
Now you've earned the right to for more freedom.
And the reality is that we have to build our trading business inside of market principles and rules no matter how much money we've made in the past.
We have to build our business inside of market principles and rules and those market principles and rules are decided by mother market.
And they they stand the test of time.
There are always the rules that the market makes us follow no matter how much money we've made in the past.
And when we get away from them that's when we get ourselves in trouble.
And so you know the other thing that we have to recognize is that just because we've had a lot of success in the past doesn't earn us the right for it to continue.
There are lots of traders that I know who've had periods of success pretty good periods of success in marketplace.
And then after that period we're eliminated by the market.
I've seen traders who've made seven figures for a pretty good stretch of time.
And then when that time is over could never make another dime in the marketplace for the rest of time. They were just done.
And we sort of we sort of have to understand that you know we should be grateful for what we can take out of the marketplace.

But we have to understand if we're going to continue being good at this that we need to be humble and we need to be learning and we need to be adapting because market regimes change and stocks trade differently.
And the world changes.
I mean this is a period of time where there is so much change around us and we can't expect everything to continue the way it was.
And so think about that. You've got to but no matter how good you are as a trader you've got to build your trading business inside of market principles and rules.
Number seven being in your seat for the easy market is how you really get rich.
Don't let hard markets or bad trading stocks or products keep you from being around for the easy markets.
There's been some tough trading products and commodities right now. Those days don't have to continue.
I think the market has been slow of late. We will have periods of that over a 10 year cycle.
You're going to have 10 great trading years where you make way too much money. More money than you deserve to make relative to your skill.
Money that you can live on for a long period of time if you're a profitable trader.
Years where it's just really a lot of fun to go and it's remarkable how well you can do.
And then there's going to be 18 months to two years which are pretty hard.
And there's going to be three good years and there's going to be three very good years which are really worth being around for.
But you've got to make it to those you've got to make it to those years.
All right number eight shark and drunken miller shark from our desk and drunken miller legendary hedge fund trader.
Probably the best performance of anyone to do it in the hedge fund space.
They both push risk when they're trading well and making money.
Your cure for getting out of a drawdown is to focus only on one thing.
I just wrote a tweet about this. I just wrote a couple of g chats to several guys in our desk reinforcing the focus.
Consistency. That's your cure when you're in a drawdown focus on consistency.
What are your best traits? What do you trade best?
What are your best entries for them? What are your best exits for them? What's the best time frame for them?
Do that and then do that again. Focus on your strengths and focus on what's made you money in the past.
And then set there and remind yourself and remind yourself and set alerts through the day to remind yourself to do what you do best.
To take the setups in your playbook that you trade best.
To take the setups in your playbook that are easiest for you.
To take the easy money for you in the marketplace.
Whatever that is and we're all different.
And there are traders that are going through a painful period. This is not fun for many traders.
It sucks. It hurts. And I think that's good.
I think that's good because that pain, people say, after the trading session when you're in a drawdown,
when you're going through painful periods, just go home and forget about it.
You can't really do that.
And I think during serious drawdowns, I don't think you should.
I think it's good to sit in that pain.
That pain is going to get you to be super focused to make sure that you understand what you do best.
That pain is going to get you to be super focused on making sure you exit and enter at the best times for you.
To make sure you don't let open profits just whittle away.
That you take those profits.
That make sure you don't miss those entries. That you get in, laser focus at the right time.
That you're in the right stocks. That you're in the right setups. That you take your best trades.
That pain forces you to not do anything else but do that.
Markets go through periods of tough times.
And I got to call the other day from a partner at a pro firm. I have to laugh at myself.
And this person, this partner, I don't know, I don't know.
I wanted to talk business and partnering and made the mistake of first asking me how things were going.
And I spent 20 minutes on the changes.
Several traders needed to make at our firm and what we needed to do to help them.
After I came up for air, I recognized that's not why this person I called.
But we're all just so consumed with getting out of holes and getting back on track and getting back to being consistent.
Making sure our guys are learning and have the resources and support they need during periods like this.
And those periods are hard.
And there had been some tough trading products when playing for a grand slam.
But, but, but, but if you're learning a new product and learning from a drawdown, this will make you a better trader.
A, your playbook will be more comprehensive.
B, your ability to trade more products will pay dividends in the future.
C, you'll reinforce the importance of building your trading business within market principles and guardrails.
D, your trading skills will improve.
Often the hardest markets, bad trading products, the most painful trading times, propel elite traders to even new heights.
We've seen that on our desk.
The key is learning, making adjustments and staying in the game.
At SMB, we're looking forward to a huge celebratory dinner for when our traders who were in drawdown reached new highs.
That's going to bring a huge sense of relief for me and for Spencer and happiness and happiness that our traders grew from that pain.
Here's to many more years of trading success for you from all of us at SMB Train and Trade Well.
So you're an active trader not doing as well as you want, not doing as well as you deserve.
And you just can't figure out why you can't become profitable no matter how hard to try.
Well, let me show you why.
This is your competition. The traders in this room.
This room right here is full of elite traders, some of whom are making seven and even eight figures a year.
In fact, our top guys have made nearly 20 million each and net trading profits in a single year.
Let's head to my office so I can share more.
So you're probably used to seeing videos of lavish trader lifestyles, trading gurus, trading off of the laptop for an hour a day, heck maybe even 15 minutes a day.
And then then relaxing on some secluded beach for the rest of the day.
Well, all I can tell you is that our traders train like pro athletes.
They live and breathe the markets and are continually working on their trading skills because at our firm, that's what we found.
It really takes to make it in this game.
I'm Mike Bellafiri co-founder and managing partner of SMB Capital, one of the world's top proprietary trading firms located in Midtown Manhattan.
And we're always looking for trading talent to hire and develop and not just to trade in house on our desk, but also to trade from their own home entirely using our firm's capital.
And we have new merst traders doing just that, allowing them to make upwards of seven figures trading the firm's capital without risking their own money.
But to even get a shot at something like that, you need to have the right training.

That's why we're doing a new three online presentation in which we share how you can get an interview with SMB to become an in house, a remote trader trading firm capital without risking yours and getting access to all of our firms coaching and resources.
And the best part, you don't have to be a profitable trader yet.
In fact, we prefer to mold profitable traders with our methods and our techniques.
That's why we have just three simple criteria that can earn anyone in an interview.
We're looking for highly ambitious and determined traders who fit our culture first and foremost.
So if you believe that could be you, sign up for the three one hour online presentation by clicking the link that's in your top right corner of your screen now.
You
